United States Department of State

Washingion, D.C. 20520

INSTRUMENT OF ACCESSION ON BEHALE OF

THE GOVERNMENT OF THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA

WHEREAS the Arrangement for the Creation in Paris of an du Vin signed at Paris on November 29,

States to

that a theountry not a signatory to the Arrangement may

to the same

proposals,

other decisions of the O.I.V.V.

pursuant to Article 1(e) of said Arrangement.

ONE at Washington this seventh da

Kan day. Shut 1994

Secretary of State

24 JA:. 1984

ET GROSKISATIOKS ITERATIORALS

No. 13

The Embassy of the United States of America presents its compliments to the Ministry of External

Relations of the Republic of France and has the honor to inform the Ministry that the Government of the

United States of America hereby applies for member- ship in the Office International de la Vigne et du

Vin in conformity with the protocol laid down by

Article VI of the Arrangement of November 29, 1924, establishing an Office International de la Vigne et du Vin in Paris.

The Government of the United States of America reserves its position concerning acceptance of any proposals, proceedings, or other decisions of the

Office International de la Vigne et du Vin undertaken pursuant to Article 1(e) of said Arrangement. In particular, the Government of the United States of

America will not accept as obligatory such proposals, proceedings, or other decisions.

The Embassy has the honor to request that the application of the Government of the United States of America be favorably considered by the Government

of France and be conveyed by it to the Governments of the other member countries for their consideration in accordance with the Arrangement.

The Embassy of the United States of America avails itself of this opportunity to renew to the Ministry of

External Relations of the Republic of France the assurances of its highest consideration.

Embassy of the United States of America

Paris, January 24, 1984
